articles.title: Why Small Habits Beat Big Resolutions Every Time
articles.excerpt: Most people try to change everything at once — and fail. Discover why tiny, consistent actions compound into life-changing results.
articles.author: Aayushya Aggarwal

articles.title: The Hidden Cost of Overthinking (And How It Quietly Holds You Back)
articles.excerpt: Overthinking feels productive. But somewhere between the second and third loop of the same thought, planning quietly becomes paralysis.
articles.author: Aayushya Aggarwal

articles.title: How AI is Helping Us Fall Back in Love with Nature
articles.excerpt: What if, instead of pulling us away from nature, technology could help us see it more clearly than ever before?

articles.title: From Data to Discovery: How AI is Making Nature Accessible to Everyone
articles.excerpt: Nature has always been complex. AI-powered tools are collapsing the knowledge barrier, turning anyone with a smartphone into a casual naturalist.
articles.author: Trackzio Team

articles.title: The Simplicity Advantage: Why Less Is Winning in Tech
articles.excerpt: Technology is becoming more powerful every day. Yet the products people love most are rarely the most complex — they're the simplest.
articles.author: Aayushya Aggarwal

articles.title: The Psychology Behind Great Apps: Why Some Products Just Feel Right
articles.excerpt: Have you ever opened an app and immediately felt at ease? That's not a happy accident — that's psychology applied deliberately.
articles.author: Aayushya Aggarwal

articles.title: Building Trackzio: Why Great Products Are Never Finished
articles.excerpt: Launch day is just the beginning. The real work starts the moment real users touch what you've built and show you what you missed.

articles.title: What Goes Into Every Update: The Thinking You Don't See
articles.excerpt: Behind every change — even a small one — there's a long chain of observation, debate, decision-making, and testing.
articles.author: Trackzio Team

articles.title: How to Stay Consistent Even When You Don't Feel Motivated
articles.excerpt: You won't feel motivated every day. The problem isn't the low days — it's what most people do on them.

articles.title: How to Manage Your Time When Everything Feels Urgent
articles.excerpt: Most people have enough time. What they don't have is clarity about which things actually deserve their attention.
